column1: # DELIBERATELY NOT DECLARED IN Files.tsv — this file is here on purpose.
# It is a data file (.tsv) that no Files.tsv row declares, so on every tutorial
# run TabuLua warns and SKIPS it:
#   WARN  Data file not listed in Files.tsv, so NOT loaded: <...>/DraftItem.tsv
#         (add a row for it to Files.tsv, or remove the file)
# Skipped means skipped: it is not parsed, not type-checked, not reformatted in
# place, and not exported. Nothing below this comment block is ever read — the
# columns need not even be valid (`sharpness:blade_edge` is not a real type, and
# no error is reported for it, because the file never reaches the parser).
# That is the point of the warning. Without it, a file you forgot to declare —
# or a half-finished draft, or a stray copy — would quietly load with whatever
# its header happened to say, and none of the wiring Files.tsv supplies (load
# order, joins, validators, pre-processors) would apply to it.
# To make this a real file, delete this comment block and add a row for it to
# Files.tsv. To silence the warning without loading it, delete the file (or move
